title: varlink: BuildImage() cannot build untagged image cleanly
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Take any `Dockerfile`
2. Use varlink's `BuildImage()` API with no `output` set

**Describe the results you received:**
`podman` successfully completes the image build and streams logs. However, at the very end - it returns an error `input name is blank`. See the log output below:
```
STEP 1: FROM docker.io/ubuntu
STEP 2: COMMIT
--> 94e814e2efa8845d95b2112d54497fbad173e45121ce9255b93401392f538499
input name is blank
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
Expected `BuildImage()` finish with a success - similarly to `podman build`. See the log output below:
```
STEP 1: FROM docker.io/ubuntu
STEP 2: COMMIT
--> 94e814e2efa8845d95b2112d54497fbad173e45121ce9255b93401392f538499
```

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
The error seems to occur at the very end of the API call implementation. It happens because logic uses [config.Output](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/pkg/varlinkapi/images.go#L257) to resolve the image ID.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:            1.3.2-dev
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.12.4
Git Commit:         25f8c21ea8ac1994cd1e8468c72e3d3481be8102
Built:              Sun May 26 14:44:11 2019
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
debug:
  compiler: gc
  git commit: 25f8c21ea8ac1994cd1e8468c72e3d3481be8102
  go version: go1.12.4
  podman version: 1.3.2-dev
host:
  BuildahVersion: 1.9.0-dev
  Conmon:
    package: 'conmon: /usr/libexec/crio/conmon'
    path: /usr/libexec/crio/conmon
    version: 'conmon version , commit: '
  Distribution:
    distribution: ubuntu
    version: "19.04"
  MemFree: 220688384
  MemTotal: 8301178880
  OCIRuntime:
    package: 'cri-o-runc: /usr/bin/runc'
    path: /usr/bin/runc
    version: 'runc version spec: 1.0.1-dev'
  SwapFree: 2045505536
  SwapTotal: 2147479552
  arch: amd64
  cpus: 4
  hostname: <redacted>
  kernel: 5.0.0-15-generic
  os: linux
  rootless: false
  uptime: 12h 8m 34.07s (Approximately 0.50 days)
registries:
  blocked: null
  insecure: null
  search: null
store:
  ConfigFile: /etc/containers/storage.conf
  ContainerStore:
    number: 14
  GraphDriverName: overlay
  GraphOptions: null
  GraphRoot: /var/lib/containers/storage
  GraphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: extfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "true"
    Supports d_type: "true"
    Using metacopy: "false"
  ImageStore:
    number: 21
  RunRoot: /var/run/containers/storage
  VolumePath: /var/lib/containers/storage/volumes

```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
N/A

comments.body: i assume you are calling directly into the endpoint using the varlink utility?  for the sake of replication, can you provide me with the exact command you are using?
I'm using the endpoint via Go API. What should I provide in this case?
i need some way to replicate what you are doing ... is it a piece of code you can share and i can use? or is it in github?

I have the code [here](https://github.com/EricHripko/dipod/blob/master/images.go#L76), but it may be quite messy (sorry). I'll try to make CLI equivalent of this `varlink` call and add it to the ticket when I have the time.
@EricHripko can you give me an exact reproducer? i.e. attach a tarball ?
you must pass the *output* attribute for this to work correctly.  I have updated the API documentation as such.
